body.text: Stablecoin on-chain volume has hit $7.5T with market cap at $316.7B, as GENIUS Act capital, reserve and licensing rules for issuers were finalized on July 18.
GENIUS Act stablecoin rulemaking deadline lands
Six federal agencies finalized capital, reserve, and licensing rules for USDC, USDT and other issuers on July 18, setting the regulatory bar for compliant stablecoins.
Tether risks USDT delisting from US exchanges
About 25% of USDT reserves remain non-compliant with GENIUS Act cash-and-Treasuries requirements; full delisting from US exchanges looms by 2028 if unresolved.
USDC overtakes USDT in transaction volume
Circle's USDC captured ~70% of adjusted stablecoin transaction volume in H1 2026 versus Tether's ~25%, aided by Circle's new OCC national trust bank approval.
Attackers stole roughly 515.2M NIGHT tokens (~$10M) from Wanchain's bridge treasury in four transactions over an eight-minute window on July 21.
A compromised oracle private key let an attacker sign future-dated fake price reports, triggering a fraudulent ~$24M USDC payout from Ostium's RWA perps vault on Arbitrum.
'Ill Bloom' vulnerability drains over $5M from wallets
A newly disclosed exploit chain dubbed 'Ill Bloom' has been used to siphon more than $5 million from cryptocurrency wallets in the past week.
Drift Protocol hit for $286M in suspected DPRK attack
Blockchain analytics firm Elliptic linked a $286M exploit of Solana-based Drift Protocol to North Korea-affiliated threat actors, one of 2026's largest DeFi hacks.
